description: Struct-key map literal — v1 codegen-deferred (§59.3 M-cut). The literal [ {a:1}: 5 ] uses a STRUCT key. A primitive-key literal ships in v1, but a struct/enum-key literal is parse-accepted, codegen-deferred to the .insert form, and surfaces W-MAP-STRUCT-KEY-LITERAL naming .insert (a documented-on-purpose v1 scope-cut, not a silent gap). Asserts CODE PRESENCE only: §59.11 states Info severity but impl#1 emits 'warning' (severity-label divergence flagged to the PA).
rationale: §59.3/§59.12 a struct/enum-key map literal is parse-accepted but codegen-deferred to .insert in v1; the compiler surfaces W-MAP-STRUCT-KEY-LITERAL naming the .insert form.
